channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Psychedelic Shooter Tamashika Hits PC and Consoles Next Month
item.content:encoded:
EDGLRD, the studio created by filmmaker Harmony Korine, has announced the release date for its debut game, psychedelic shooter Tamashika.

Per a press release, Tamashika will be launching on PC and consoles on April 10th, and EDGLRD, along with developer quicktequila, released a brand new trailer providing a fresh glimpse into the game's multi-colored world as well.

Tamashika has been in the works for several years at this point; in a recent blog post, one of the game's developers revealed that the game has been in development for three years "across seven countries in total", so it's been a real labor of love for those working on it.

EDGLRD and quicktequila describe Tamashika as a "psychedelic trance of shifting corridors" in which you'll need to use your semi-automatic pistol and tanto blade to navigate ever-changing mazes.

Unlike many other roguelikes of its kind, Tamashika doesn't allow you to change loadouts or try new builds; you've got one set of weapons, and thus "no excuses", as the two studios say.

Tamashika's levels will change once per day, and although there aren't any loadouts or ways to optimize your build, you will find leaderboards on which you can compete for the highest score. This is very much an experiential game along the lines of Devil Daggers or Post Void.

You can check out a new trailer for Tamashika, which shows off the game's psychedelic art style and fast-paced gameplay, right here.

Tamashika has a pretty hefty pedigree behind it in Korine's EDGLRD; the filmmaker is known for movies like Kids, Gummo, and Spring Breakers, although he's yet to prove himself in the world of gaming.

If you want to see whether his debut effort as a video game publisher bears fruit, then you can check out Tamashika on PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch. It's launching on April 10th.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/psychedelic-shooter-tamashika-hits-pc-and-consoles-next-month
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marvel Rivals Season 7 Introduces Black Cat and White Fox This Week
item.content:encoded:
NetEase has announced the release date for Season 7 of the popular hero shooter Marvel Rivals, and it's bringing two iconic new heroes with it later this week.

As revealed in a brand new trailer, Season 7, which (as you've probably already guessed) follows on from the game's sixth season, and it'll bring Black Cat and White Fox into the fold as playable characters. The season kicks off on March 20th.

NetEase has also revealed that White Fox will be a Strategist, joining a roster that also includes the likes of Gambit and Ultron. We don't yet know what role Black Cat will play, but if I had to guess, I'd say she's going to be a Duelist (damage-dealer).

The story in Season 7 will revolve around forces from 2099 invading modern New York, leading mayor Wilson Fisk (better known as the Kingpin) to take drastic measures to resist the changes coming to his city.

Presumably, since it's been revealed which role White Fox will take, she'll be the first hero released as part of Season 7, with Black Cat following on in the second half of the season's cycle.

If you're not familiar with Marvel Rivals, it's a hero shooter along the lines of games like Overwatch, although, as the name suggests, its roster is filled with iconic Marvel heroes like Captain America, Spider-Man, and Phoenix, among many others.

The game sees teams of heroes taking to various maps in order to contest objectives, with each hero filling one of the three core roles most hero shooters espouse; tanks (Vanguards), damage-dealers (Duelists), and healers and support characters (Strategists).

Despite the fact that Marvel Rivals has met with widespread success, the original development team for the game was unexpectedly closed down last February, although naturally, a support team remains to keep the game updated and to introduce new heroes.

Marvel Rivals is available to play for free right now on PC, PlayStation, and Xbox Series X|S. The game's seventh season arrives on March 20th.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/marvel-rivals-season-7-release-date-black-cat-white-fox
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Starfield PS5 Release Date Confirmed for April Alongside Major Update and New DLC
item.content:encoded:
Bethesda has revealed the PS5 release date for spacefaring RPG Starfield, and you can get ready to experience the studio's latest open-world adventure next month.

As revealed in an official Bethesda blog post, Starfield will be coming to PS5 on April 7th, and it'll arrive alongside major free update Free Lanes and a brand new story DLC by the name of Terran Armada.

Of course, the PlayStation 5 version of Starfield will also launch with all of the updates that have been released for the game so far, so if you've only played the launch version on other platforms, this might be a good way to get started.

The reveal of this release date lines up with a leak from earlier this month. Contrary to Todd Howard's comments this year, the Free Lanes update arguably does represent a sort of Starfield 2.0, although it's definitely not transformative in terms of what kind of game you're getting.

Free Lanes introduces the ability to free-fly between planets in a solar system, as well as Cruise Mode, which allows you to chat with your companions or work on your ship while you're in transit. Bethesda is also promising new points of interest in space, which you can redirect to if you want to check them out.

A new resource, X-Tech, allows you to customize your gear even further, and a number of other quality-of-life changes and improvements have been made across systems like Outposts, Starborn abilities, and more.

The new story DLC, meanwhile, sees you taking on a new faction consisting of "members of the United Colonies and Freestar Collective who vanished during the Colony War". It'll set you back $9.99 and bring a new companion to the experience as well.

Starfield launches on PS5 on April 7th, alongside the Free Lanes update and the Terran Armada story DLC (which will be available on PC and Xbox Series X|S as well). Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
